item.title: 01-TRACE-A-REQUEST-END-TO-END
item.description: Trace A Request End To End The fastest way to learn SaaS infrastructure is to follow one request until it becomes boring.

item.title: 02-NETWORKING-HTTP-DNS-TLS
item.description: Networking, HTTP, DNS, And TLS For SaaS work, you do not need to become a router engineer.

item.title: 03-AUTH-SESSIONS-COOKIES-OAUTH
item.description: Authentication, Sessions, Cookies, And OAuth Auth feels puzzling because several questions are often collapsed into one word.

item.title: 04-BACKEND-APIS-AND-SERVICE-DESIGN
item.description: Backend Shape, APIs, And Service Design A SaaS backend is a set of contracts around state.

item.title: 05-STATE-DATA-CACHES-AND-QUEUES
item.description: State, Data, Caches, And Queues State is what makes distributed systems hard.

item.title: 06-CLOUD-CONTAINERS-AND-DEPLOYMENT
item.description: Cloud, Containers, And Deployment Cloud infrastructure is rented primitives plus automation.

item.title: 07-SECURITY-THREAT-MODEL
item.description: Security Threat Model For Builders Security is not a separate department-shaped spell.

item.title: 08-OBSERVABILITY-RELIABILITY-OPERATIONS
item.description: Observability, Reliability, And Operations Modern SaaS is not done when the code works locally.

item.title: 09-PRODUCT-ENGINEERING-AND-SCALING
item.description: Product Engineering And Scaling Judgment Modern SaaS infrastructure exists to serve product learning, not to cosplay as a distributed systems paper.

item.title: 10-PRACTICE-PLAYBOOKS
item.description: Practice Playbooks And Meeting Drills The goal is not to memorize product names. The goal is to build reflexes.
